title: Agent-seamlessness residuals: first-guess CLI, gate-output readability, and validate scoping
description:
Close the validated friction residuals that make AI agents fight the `jit` CLI: first-guess commands that fail, gate review reports read in truncated slices (driving multi-round review churn), a per-issue validation gate that reds out the whole repo, and a verdict parser that silently records clean passes as failures.

## Background

A mining pass over 36 Claude sessions (384 incidents, 11 themes) was re-validated against the current binary in isolated repos. A prior session-mining epic already shipped a large slice (noun/flag aliases, gate exit-code taxonomy, skip-pass-at-HEAD, `short_id`/`labels` in listings, claim release-by-id). This epic delivers the verified remainder: the first-guess command surface, full gate-output readability, review-churn reduction, the verdict-parser fix, two-tier validation, and the missing maintenance command. The full audit, the per-item evidence, and the reproduction battery live in the planning node's plan doc.

## Success Criteria

- [hard] REQ-01: `jit issue create "Title"` accepts the title positionally (the existing flag form remains valid).
- [hard] REQ-02: `jit issue create` and `jit issue update` accept a typed `--type <kind>` that records a validated type label, with the kind still required to be declared in config.
- [hard] REQ-03: `jit gate pass` and `jit gate check` accept the gate as a flag in addition to the positional key.
- [hard] REQ-04: `jit query ready` resolves to the available-work query, and bare `jit query` returns all issues instead of erroring, with the existing `query all` filters reachable on that default path.
- [hard] REQ-05: The remaining first-guess command/flag failures are removed, covering at least: the gate-mode flag on gate definition, the document-add label/type flags, the gate-inspection key-versus-issue-id confusion, and top-level guesses for reverse-dependencies and issue listing. (Excludes the noun/flag aliases already shipped.)
- [hard] REQ-06: A single documented command grammar governs the whole CLI: consistent positional-versus-flag conventions, consistent id-acceptance semantics across sibling commands, consistent noun/verb structure, and a clear separation of gate configuration (registry definition, editing, removal, attachment) from gate execution and inspection. The grammar is written down as the standard and applied across all commands; every command that deviates is renamed to its canonical form, with all consumers (MCP-generated tools, `../gf2`, the jit-* skills, and docs) updated in the same change.
- [hard] REQ-07: Gate inspection shows the full checker report rather than a silently truncated fragment followed by a file path.
- [hard] REQ-08: Gate inspection and run history are one command surface: the latest run by default, prior runs listable and filterable by gate and status, and an unwrapped report-text view suitable for direct reading.
- [hard] REQ-09: The code-review gate instructs the reviewer to emit, in a single pass, a numbered enumeration of every finding across all categories with an explicit total count.
- [hard] REQ-10: The breakdown and review skill prose direct the worker to read the complete review report and account for every numbered finding before re-requesting the gate, and ensure breakdown output ships gated, descriptively-titled, type-derived items meeting the content standards.
- [hard] REQ-11: The AI-review verdict is parsed robustly so that reviewer prose following the verdict line no longer records a passing review as a failure, fixed in every copy of the checker and protected by a regression test.
- [hard] REQ-12: The per-issue validation gate validates only the issue under review, so repository-wide integrity findings on unrelated issues no longer fail the gate.
- [hard] REQ-13: A rigorous, config-declared repository-wide integrity check runs at container completion, so scoping the per-issue gate cannot let global validation debt accumulate silently.
- [hard] REQ-14: `jit issue create --json` emits the same issue contract as `jit issue show --json` — including the short id, the per-gate `gates` array (replacing the legacy `gates_required`/`gates_status` fields), and enriched dependency and label arrays — so the two commands agree on shape.
- [hard] REQ-15: The exported schema for the dependency-graph command reports the shape the command actually emits.
- [hard] REQ-16: An existing gate's mutable fields can be edited through the CLI with an atomic write that preserves per-issue gate status, without hand-editing the registry file.
- [hard] REQ-17: Claim and lease commands emit a clear, actionable error when run outside a git repository, and the git requirement for claims is documented, including amending the project's "git is optional" guidance to record the lease exception.
- [hard] REQ-18: Claim and lease skill prose distinguishes assignment from advisory leases, prescribes assign-only for dependency-blocked containers, and clarifies that the worktree active-claims count reflects leases rather than assignments.
- [hard] REQ-19: The recurring transitive-dependency request on the graph commands is resolved by an explicit decision and implementation that does not enable unbounded graph output by default; in particular the reverse-dependency command no longer defaults to unbounded depth.

## Notes

- CLI consistency is a governing principle of this epic, not a cleanup afterthought: REQ-06 defines and applies one command grammar, and REQ-01..05 are specific instances of it. Changes favor clean renames to canonical forms over compatibility shims; the single real consumer set (`../gf2`, the jit-* skills, the MCP-generated tools, and docs) is updated in the same change as each rename.
- Type validity (REQ-02) is resolved through the existing config and validation layers; no domain type literal is hardcoded into command or engine code.
- Generalizes and extends the completed agent-ergonomics epic (`53e3fa36`); this epic owns only the verified residuals that epic did not ship. The gate-output items (REQ-07/08) deliberately revisit that epic's decision to omit run history and inline output, because session evidence showed agents could not in practice read reports through the paths it assumed.
- Out of scope: a jit-owned gate-run serialization lock (existing host-wide serialization suffices); an engine-level required-gates validation rule (addressed via skill prose and the review prompt); making claims work without git (the requirement is accepted and documented); a blind reintroduction of the removed transitive flag.
- Decomposition, the full decision log, and per-item evidence live in the planning node's plan doc; each `REQ-NN` is credited by a breakdown child carrying `satisfies:REQ-NN`.
